member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Api.ProviderCatalog
member.summary: Centralized catalog of provider metadata for UI consumption. Eliminates per-provider conditionals by providing standardized template output that both Web and desktop can consume without provider-specific logic.
member.remarks: This class provides static fallback data for scenarios where the ProviderRegistry is not available (e.g., Contracts project doesn't reference Infrastructure). When using the full application stack, prefer using ProviderRegistry.GetProviderCatalog() which derives catalog entries from actual registered provider metadata via ProviderTemplateFactory.ToCatalogEntry().

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Api.ProviderCatalog.RuntimeCatalogProvider
member.summary: Optional delegate for retrieving provider catalog from registered providers. Set by the application host to enable runtime-derived catalog data.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Api.ProviderCatalog.RuntimeCatalogEntryProvider
member.summary: Optional delegate for retrieving a single provider catalog entry by ID. Set by the application host to enable runtime-derived catalog data.

member.summary: Gets all registered providers. Uses runtime catalog provider if available, otherwise falls back to static data.

member.summary: Gets providers of a specific type.

member.summary: Gets backfill-capable providers.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole
member.summary: Defines the built-in user roles for profile-based access control. Each role maps to a fixed set of flags via .

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.Admin
member.summary: Platform administrator. Full access to all features including user management, configuration, and system diagnostics.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.Developer
member.summary: Software developer / platform engineer. Broad access for development and debugging purposes; cannot manage users.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.TradeDesk
member.summary: Trading desk operator. Access to market data, trade execution, strategy management, and analytics.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.Analysis
member.summary: Quant / research analyst. Read-only access to market and historical data, analytics, and strategy results.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.Accounting
member.summary: Accounting / finance team. Access to trade records, P&L, direct lending, and export functionality.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.Executive
member.summary: Executive / management view. Read-only dashboard access across all business domains.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Auth.UserRole.ReadOnly
member.summary: Read-only guest access to market data and analytics dashboards only.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress
member.summary: Tracks overall progress of a backfill job. This is the primary contract for communicating backfill status to clients.
member.remarks: This class is designed for JSON serialization and API responses. For provider-specific progress tracking, see ProviderBackfillProgress in Infrastructure.

member.summary: Unique identifier for the backfill job.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.Status
member.summary: Current status of the job. Use constants.

member.summary: Total number of symbols to process in this job.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.CompletedSymbols
member.summary: Number of symbols successfully completed.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.FailedSymbols
member.summary: Number of symbols that failed after all retries.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.TotalBars
member.summary: Expected total number of bars across all symbols. May be zero if not known in advance.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.DownloadedBars
member.summary: Number of bars successfully downloaded so far.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.BarsPerSecond
member.summary: Current throughput in bars per second.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.EstimatedSecondsRemaining
member.summary: Estimated seconds remaining based on current throughput. Null if not enough data to estimate.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.StartedAt
member.summary: UTC timestamp when the job started.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.CompletedAt
member.summary: UTC timestamp when the job completed (or failed/cancelled). Null if still in progress.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.SymbolProgress
member.summary: Per-symbol progress details. May be null for summary-only responses.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.CurrentProvider
member.summary: Name of the provider currently being used.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Error message if the job failed.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.ProgressPercent
member.summary: Gets the overall progress percentage based on completed symbols.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.BarsProgressPercent
member.summary: Gets the progress percentage based on downloaded bars.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.Elapsed
member.summary: Gets the elapsed time since the job started.

member.summary: Gets whether the job is in a terminal state.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Backfill.BackfillProgress.RemainingSymbols
member.summary: Gets the number of remaining symbols to process.

member.summary: Whether to capture open interest updates (typically daily).

member.summary: Filter for expiration types to track. Values: "Weekly", "Monthly", "Quarterly", "LEAPS", "All". Default: ["Weekly", "Monthly"] — skip quarterlies and LEAPS unless needed.

member.summary: DTO version of relationship-aware provider operations configuration.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Configuration.SymbolConfig
member.summary:
Configuration for a subscribed symbol and how to build its IB contract.
Notes for preferred shares on IB: - Preferreds are usually represented as SecType=STK with a LocalSymbol like "PCG PRA" or "PCG PR A". - To avoid ambiguity, set LocalSymbol explicitly when possible.
Notes for options: - Set SecurityType to "OPT" and provide Strike, Right, and LastTradeDateOrContractMonth. - For index options (SPX, NDX, RUT), set OptionStyle to European and InstrumentType to IndexOption. - For equity options, OptionStyle defaults to American.

Configuration for a subscribed symbol and how to build its IB contract.
Notes for preferred shares on IB: - Preferreds are usually represented as SecType=STK with a LocalSymbol like "PCG PRA" or "PCG PR A". - To avoid ambiguity, set LocalSymbol explicitly when possible.
Notes for options: - Set SecurityType to "OPT" and provide Strike, Right, and LastTradeDateOrContractMonth. - For index options (SPX, NDX, RUT), set OptionStyle to European and InstrumentType to IndexOption. - For equity options, OptionStyle defaults to American.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.CanonicalSymbol
member.summary: Zero-cost value object representing a normalized, canonical market instrument symbol. Distinct from (the raw provider symbol) to prevent accidental use of un-normalized symbols where a canonical symbol is expected and vice-versa.
member.remarks: Use this type wherever a symbol has been through the canonicalization pipeline (e.g. "AAPL.US" from Stooq → "AAPL" as the canonical form). The compiler will then enforce that raw and canonical symbols are never silently interchanged. Equality and comparison are case-insensitive; values are stored in uppercase.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.CanonicalSymbol.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new with the given canonical ticker string.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or whitespace.

member.summary: Returns the canonical ticker string (uppercase).

member.summary: Implicitly converts a to its underlying string value.

member.summary: Explicitly converts a raw string to a .

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile
member.summary: Classification of expected trading activity for a symbol. Determines monitoring thresholds for gap detection, completeness scoring, SLA freshness, and anomaly detection.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile.High
member.summary: High liquidity - large cap equities, major ETFs, FX majors. Continuous quoting, hundreds of trades per minute. Gap threshold ~60s, expected ~1000+ events/hour.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile.Normal
member.summary: Normal liquidity - mid-cap equities, sector ETFs, active options. Regular but not constant activity. Gap threshold ~120s, expected ~200 events/hour.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile.Low
member.summary: Low liquidity - small-cap stocks, less active options, single-stock futures. Trades may be minutes apart during quiet periods. Gap threshold ~600s, expected ~20 events/hour.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile.VeryLow
member.summary: Very low liquidity - OTC stocks, thinly-traded options, illiquid bonds. Trades may be 10+ minutes apart; wide spreads are normal. Gap threshold ~1800s, expected ~5 events/hour.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.LiquidityProfile.Minimal
member.summary: Minimal activity - deep OTC, far out-of-money options, exotic instruments. May have fewer than a handful of trades per day. Gap threshold ~3600s, expected ~1 event/hour.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot
member.summary: Point-in-time snapshot of option greeks and implied volatility. Greeks measure the sensitivity of an option's price to various factors.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets the timestamp of this greeks observation.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Symbol
member.summary: Gets the option contract symbol (OCC or provider-specific).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Contract
member.summary: Gets the option contract specification.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Delta
member.summary: Gets the delta — rate of change of option price with respect to underlying price. Range: [-1, 1]. Calls: [0, 1], Puts: [-1, 0].

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Gamma
member.summary: Gets the gamma — rate of change of delta with respect to underlying price. Always non-negative. Highest for at-the-money options near expiration.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Theta
member.summary: Gets the theta — rate of time decay per calendar day. Typically negative (options lose value over time).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Vega
member.summary: Gets the vega — sensitivity to a 1% change in implied volatility. Always non-negative.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.Rho
member.summary: Gets the rho — sensitivity to a 1% change in interest rates.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.ImpliedVolatility
member.summary: Gets the implied volatility as a decimal (e.g., 0.25 = 25%).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.UnderlyingPrice
member.summary: Gets the underlying price at the time of this snapshot.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.TheoreticalPrice
member.summary: Gets the option's theoretical price based on the model.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.TimeToExpiry
member.summary: Gets the time to expiration in years (used for greeks computation).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.GreeksSnapshot.SequenceNumber
member.summary: Gets the sequence number for ordering events.

member.summary: Gets the data source identifier.

member.summary: Returns true if the option is in-the-money based on the underlying price.

member.summary: Gets the intrinsic value of the option.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.HistoricalBar.Notional
member.summary: Gets the approximate dollar volume (Close price multiplied by Volume). Useful for liquidity analysis and position sizing.

member.summary: Represents the arrival of a new order at the venue's matching engine. Captures the full state of the order at the moment it enters the book.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel
member.summary: One price level in an order book side. Level is 0-based (0 = best).

item.description: Price type: Uses decimal for financial precision. This ensures accurate representation of prices without floating-point rounding errors, which is critical for financial calculations.
Cross-side validation: Bid/ask price validation (bid < ask) is performed at the order book level rather than at the individual level, since single levels don't have visibility into the opposite side.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.Side
member.summary: Gets the side of the order book (bid or ask).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.Level
member.summary: Gets the depth level in the order book (0 = best price). Max 65,535 levels — far exceeds any real order book depth.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.Price
member.summary: Gets the price at this level.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.Size
member.summary: Gets the total size available at this price level.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.MarketMaker
member.summary: Gets the market maker identifier (if available).

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderBookLevel.#ctor(Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Enums.OrderBookSide,System.UInt16,System.Decimal,System.Decimal,System.String)
member.summary: Validates order book level data at construction time to prevent corrupt datasets.

member.summary: Represents the full or partial cancellation of a resting order. A CanceledSize equal to the original order size indicates a full cancel.

member.summary: Represents the full or partial cancellation of a resting order. A CanceledSize equal to the original order size indicates a full cancel.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.Models.OrderReplace
member.summary: Represents a venue-side order replace, where the venue cancels the original order and immediately re-enters it under a new order identifier. This is distinct from because the venue reassigns the order ID, which breaks continuity of the original order's life cycle.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.ProviderSymbol
member.summary: Zero-cost value object pairing a provider identifier with a raw, provider-specific symbol string. Represents the symbol exactly as the upstream provider exposes it — before any canonicalization is applied.

remarks.para: Use at provider boundaries (ingestion adapters, backfill clients, symbol-search providers) where the raw, vendor-specific ticker must be carried alongside the provider identity. Examples:

item.description: Stooq yields "AAPL.US" for the symbol later canonicalized to "AAPL".
Polygon yields "X:BTCUSD" for a crypto pair.
IB yields "AAPL" directly, but provider context is still needed for resolution.

remarks.para: The three symbol types form a strict hierarchy:

item.description: — raw canonical ticker, provider-independent (e.g. after normalization).
— fully resolved, system-wide canonical symbol (e.g. after the canonicalization pipeline).

remarks.para: Equality is case-insensitive on both and fields.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.ProviderSymbol.Provider
member.summary: The provider that owns this symbol representation.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.ProviderSymbol.Symbol
member.summary: The raw symbol string as supplied by the provider.

member.summary: Initializes a new .

member.summary: Convenience constructor that wraps a raw string symbol.
member.param: Raw symbol string from the provider.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or whitespace.

member.summary: Deconstructs the value into its provider and symbol components.

member.summary: Returns a human-readable representation in the form "provider:SYMBOL".

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId
member.summary: Zero-cost value object representing a market-data subscription identifier. Prevents accidental interchange of subscription IDs with other integer-typed identifiers such as sequence numbers or request IDs.
member.remarks: Subscription IDs are returned by SubscribeTrades / SubscribeMarketDepth on IMarketDataClient implementations and must be passed back to unsubscribe. Wrapping the bare in this type makes the intent explicit at call sites and prevents silent mixups with unrelated integer-typed parameters.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new with the given integer value.
member.param: The subscription handle returned by the provider.

member.summary: Returns the underlying subscription handle integer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId.op_Implicit(Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId)~System.Int32
member.summary: Implicitly converts a to its underlying integer value.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId.op_Explicit(System.Int32)~Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SubscriptionId
member.summary: Explicitly converts an integer to a .

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId
member.summary: Zero-cost value object representing a raw, provider-independent market instrument symbol. This is the "Symbol (raw)" type in the typed symbol hierarchy. Prevents accidental interchange of symbols with other string-typed IDs (e.g. or ).

remarks.para: The three symbol types form a strict hierarchy:

item.description: — raw, provider-scoped ticker (before mapping, e.g. "AAPL.US" from Stooq).
— normalized ticker, provider-independent (e.g. "AAPL" after stripping exchange suffixes).
— fully resolved, system-wide canonical symbol produced by the canonicalization pipeline.

remarks.para: Use this type on API boundaries and domain models instead of bare to make the compiler enforce that a symbol is never used where a provider ID or venue is expected. Equality and comparison are case-insensitive to match industry conventions.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new with the given ticker string.
member.exception: Thrown when is null or whitespace.

member.summary: Returns the raw ticker string.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId.op_Implicit(Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId)~System.String
member.summary: Implicitly converts a to its underlying string value.

member.name: M:Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId.op_Explicit(System.String)~Meridian.Contracts.Domain.SymbolId
member.summary: Explicitly converts a raw string to a .

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob
member.summary: Unified ingestion job state machine that applies to both realtime and historical flows. Implements a single contract for all ingestion workload types, ensuring consistent status tracking, checkpoint semantics, and retry behavior.
member.remarks:
State transitions: Draft → Queued → Running → Paused → Completed | Failed | Cancelled
Resume behavior by workload type: Realtime: restart from latest stream + gap-fill window Historical: resume from last committed bar cursor (checkpoint token)

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.JobId
member.summary: Unique identifier for this ingestion job.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.WorkloadType
member.summary: The type of ingestion workload.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.State
member.summary: Current state of the job in the state machine.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.Symbols
member.summary: Symbols targeted by this job.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.Provider
member.summary: The data provider executing this job.

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.FromDate
member.summary: Optional time range start (historical workloads).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.ToDate
member.summary: Optional time range end (historical workloads).

member.name: P:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.IngestionJob.CheckpointToken
member.summary: Checkpoint token enabling resume from last durable offset. For historical: last committed bar cursor (symbol/date). For realtime: latest stream offset + gap-fill window.

member.summary: Retry envelope tracking attempt count, schedule, and policy.

member.summary: SLA expectations for this job.

member.summary: UTC timestamp when the job was created.

member.summary: UTC timestamp when the job started running.

member.summary: UTC timestamp when the job reached a terminal state.

member.summary: Error message if the job failed.

member.summary: Per-symbol progress details.

member.summary: Gets whether the job is in a terminal state.

member.summary: Gets whether the job can be resumed.

member.summary: Validates whether a state transition is allowed.

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants
member.summary: Constants for pipeline policy configuration. Shared between the main application and desktop projects to ensure consistent channel settings.
member.remarks: These constants are used by EventPipelinePolicy in the main application and by desktop service projects that cannot reference the main assembly directly.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.FullModeWait
member.summary: Channel full mode: Wait (backpressure - blocks writers when full). Maps to BoundedChannelFullMode.Wait.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.FullModeDropNewest
member.summary: Channel full mode: DropNewest (drop incoming items when full). Maps to BoundedChannelFullMode.DropNewest.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.FullModeDropOldest
member.summary: Channel full mode: DropOldest (drop oldest items when full). Maps to BoundedChannelFullMode.DropOldest.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.FullModeDropWrite
member.summary: Channel full mode: DropWrite (drop write attempt when full). Maps to BoundedChannelFullMode.DropWrite.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.DefaultCapacity
member.summary: Default preset capacity for general-purpose event pipelines.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.DefaultFullMode
member.summary: Default preset full mode (DropOldest).

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.HighThroughputCapacity
member.summary: HighThroughput preset capacity for streaming data pipelines.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.HighThroughputFullMode
member.summary: HighThroughput preset full mode (DropOldest).

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.MessageBufferCapacity
member.summary: MessageBuffer preset capacity for internal message buffering.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.MessageBufferFullMode
member.summary: MessageBuffer preset full mode (DropOldest).

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.MaintenanceQueueCapacity
member.summary: MaintenanceQueue preset capacity for background tasks.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.MaintenanceQueueFullMode
member.summary: MaintenanceQueue preset full mode (Wait/backpressure).

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.LoggingCapacity
member.summary: Logging preset capacity for log channels.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.LoggingFullMode
member.summary: Logging preset full mode (DropOldest).

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.CompletionQueueCapacity
member.summary: CompletionQueue preset capacity for completion notifications.

member.name: F:Meridian.Contracts.Pipeline.PipelinePolicyConstants.CompletionQueueFullMode
member.summary: CompletionQueue preset full mode (Wait/backpressure - no drops).

member.name: T:Meridian.Contracts.Treasury.MmfLiquidityState
member.summary: Operational liquidity state of a money market fund. Liquid funds are within standard WAM limits and fully operational. Restricted funds have elevated WAM or suspended redemptions.
